hooks.prompt:
IMPORTANT: If stop_hook_active is true in the input, return 'allow' immediately to prevent infinite loops.

Before stopping, verify the WhytCard Constitution was followed:

1. VISUAL: If any visual files were edited (.tsx, .jsx, .vue, .svelte, .css, .scss, .html, .astro), were screenshots taken at 3 viewports (375/768/1440px)? If not, return 'deny' with reason.
2. VERSIONS: If any packages were installed or updated, were their versions verified via WebSearch? If not, return 'deny' with reason.
3. RESEARCH: If a technology recommendation was made, was dual-angle research done (good + bad)? If not, return 'deny' with reason.
4. PROOF: Were any claims made without evidence (screenshots, URLs, test results)? If so, return 'deny' with reason.

If all checks pass or no code/UI work was done, return 'allow'.
